scope.summary: Implement OBJ + .mtl static mesh importer for avatar, prop, and trim source geometry into tailor_avatars / tailor_body_proxies / tailor_trims authority tables
acceptance_criteria: POST /tailor/avatars/import with a multipart OBJ + MTL payload inserts a row in tailor_avatars with source_kind='custom_obj' and emits TailorAvatarCreated
Imported mesh positions are stored in centimetres; if OBJ units are unknown, a unit-hint field in the request body overrides
MTL material names are stored in tailor_body_proxies mesh_json and forwarded to the trim import path without loss
OBJ with multiple named groups imports each group as a separate sub-mesh (tagged by group name) suitable for per-region capsule decomposition
guard_authority_write(AuthorityMode::Postgres) is called before any INSERT on tailor_avatars or tailor_body_proxies
Round-trip: export the imported avatar as OBJ sequence (MT-251 path) and assert vertex count matches within tolerance
scope.risk_if_missed: Operators and models cannot bring external body meshes (Daz, CC4, custom scans) into Tailor without a separate out-of-band pipeline step. Every avatar has to enter via the parametric system only, blocking real-world character pipelines.

risks: Broad MT scope can hide multiple review targets.
Markdown MT lists may disagree with packet-declared active/next MT truth.
Receipt routing may target an old MT if contract identity is omitted.
minimum_controls: Exactly one primary proof target per MT unless the packet records why not.
MT identity must be stable and referenced by receipt correlation.
Generated projection drift must not wake Coder or Validator by itself.
